title: FeatureManifest
description: Normative schema for a single feature manifest (Appendix A, Table 5). A manifest is a multiset of typed structural feature tokens of cardinality |f| in [32, 128], canonically ordered before MinHash projection. No field encodes raw prompt text, user identity, or model weights; this satisfies the privacy invariant at the schema level rather than at runtime. Before emission, the aggregate Shannon entropy H(f) must satisfy the minimum-entropy gate H(f) >= H_min = 24 bits (gate presence: specified; recommended H_min value: proposed). The manifest_version field identifies the schema revision under which the manifest was generated; receivers reject manifests at unsupported versions.

manifest_version.description: Schema revision identifier of the form fm-YYYY-MM. Receivers reject manifests whose version is not in their SupportedManifests set. Schema changes are announced with a deprecation window of no less than 60 days.

intent_class.description: Output of the local classifier stack. One or more of the six adversarial-class labels from Table 2, encoded as (intent_class, label_id) tokens. Cardinality: 1-3 tokens. Entropy contribution: log2(6) ~= 2.58 bits per token, uniform over 6 classes; higher in practice because the empirical distribution is non-uniform.

behavior_phase.description: Latent phase label from the trajectory classifier in the Behavioral Architecture (Sec. 6). One of the five grooming-phase labels; phase enumerations for other harm categories are governed analogously. Encoded as (behavior_phase, label_id) tokens. Cardinality: 0-2 tokens. Entropy contribution: log2(5) ~= 2.32 bits per token.

phase_transition.description: Detected phase-transition signal in the current conversation window. Encoded as (phase_transition, transition_id). Cardinality: 0-1 token. Entropy contribution: ~3 bits per token for grooming's five-state chain (10 directed transitions plus the null transition). The null transition (no transition detected) is represented by the absence of this field.

embed_anom_bin.description: Embedding-space anomaly score from the local classifier, quantized to B=16 bins. Encoded as (embed_anom_bin_k, 1) for each bin k that exceeds the participant's calibration threshold. Multiple bin tokens indicate clustered anomaly. Quantization is the canonicalization step that makes cross-provider matching tolerant of embedding-stack drift. Cardinality: 8-16 tokens. Entropy contribution: log2(16) = 4 bits per token.

role_marker.description: Presence flags for role-injection markers. Encoded as (role_marker, marker_id) for each detected marker. Cardinality: 0-6 tokens. Entropy contribution: log2(6) ~= 2.58 bits per token; up to 6 * log2(6) ~= 15.5 bits when all markers are present.

hypoth_frame.description: Hypothetical-sandboxing frame markers. Encoded as (hypoth_frame, frame_id) for each detected frame. Cardinality: 0-4 tokens. Entropy contribution: log2(5) ~= 2.32 bits per token.

decomp_signal.description: Cross-turn intent-aggregation score from the sequence classifier in the Behavioral Architecture (Sec. 6), quantized to B=8 bins per turn over a sliding window of up to k=16 turns. Encoded as (decomp_signal, turn_idx, bin) triples. Cardinality: 4-16 tokens. Entropy contribution: log2(8 * 16) = 7 bits per token.

turn_idx.description: Zero-based index of the conversation turn within the sliding window (0-15).

bin.description: Quantized anomaly bin (0-7) for this turn.

prem_drift.description: Premise-drift signal. Divergence between the structured belief-state at turn t and the belief-state at turn t-k for k in {1, 2, 4, 8}, quantized to B=8 bins. Encoded as (prem_drift, k, bin) triples. Cardinality: 0-8 tokens. Entropy contribution: log2(8 * 4) = 5 bits per token.

k.description: Turn-lag offset k in {1, 2, 4, 8} over which drift is measured.

bin.description: Quantized drift bin (0-7) for this lag offset.

provenance_fail.description: MCP / retrieval-channel provenance failure markers from the Composition Framework (Sec. 8). Encoded as (provenance_fail, marker_id) for each detected failure. Cardinality: 0-3 tokens. Entropy contribution: log2(3) ~= 1.58 bits per token.

demographic_prior.description: Inferred demographic-asymmetry prior (for example, an inferred adult-to-minor interaction), used to raise classifier sensitivity per the Behavioral Architecture (Sec. 6). Encoded as (demographic_prior, prior_id) for non-default priors only; the default prior is omitted to avoid emitting a constant token. Cardinality: 0-1 token. Entropy contribution: ~12 bits per token. The enum of recognized prior identifiers is extended through the manifest_version mechanism.

scope_class.description: Coarse harm-category gate matching the scope field of the handshake payload. Disambiguates the federated cache lookup. Encoded as (scope_class, class_id). Cardinality: exactly 1 token. Entropy contribution: log2(3) ~= 1.58 bits per token. This field is low-entropy by design; it carries no adversarial-class discriminative information.
